name: Floyd Smith
abstract: Floyd George Smith (January 25, 1917 – March 29, 1982), sometimes credited as Floyd "Guitar" Smith, was an American jazz guitarist and record producer.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: There were only three musicians in the orchestra that I knew slightly... Floyd Smith, guitar... I had however seen Andy Kirk and his 'Clouds of Joy' quite a few times... Floyd Smith was well known for his 'Floyd's Guitar Blues'...

about_quotes.speaker: Hayes Pillars
about_quotes.evidence: Floyd Smith had joined us... Floyd stayed with us until 1938.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: And if he played a solo, Floyd Smith would play it along with him as a duet. And if Floyd played a solo, he would play it along with Floyd. That's how well they were, you know.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Durham
about_quotes.evidence: The year I was with Charlie Christian, two months later, we went to Nebraska, and Floyd didn't own a guitar... So she gave him $70 and he went right down with me

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Alden
about_quotes.evidence: Floyd Smith made a record with Andy Kirk’s band called “Floyd’s Guitar Blues,” and it’s really hard to tell exactly who was the first.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Alden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Clayton Duerr
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Well you know, Floyd Smith, I don't know if you ever heard him, you used to do with Andy [INTERVIEWEE] Kirk? Yeah.
